title: Becoming a climate champion – UN CC:e-Learn course

title: Extreme weather resilience: why it matters and how we’re building it
content: This paper examines why extreme weather resilience is becoming a core priority for infrastructure investors and how it should be built into investment decisions.
snippet: This paper examines why extreme weather resilience is becoming a core priority for infrastructure investors and how it should be built into investment decisions.

title: Urban Transitions (Elsevier)

title: The credit channel of acute vs chronic climate change-related risk

title: How 65 major U.S. cities are responding to urban heat
content: Climate Central analysis shows that residents of the 65 largest U.S. cities experience an average of 8°F of extra heat due to the built environment.
snippet: Climate Central analysis shows that residents of the 65 largest U.S. cities experience an average of 8°F of extra heat due to the built environment.

title: Enhancing urban traffic resilience under pluvial flooding through adaptive signal control
content: This study developed an integrated simulation framework that couples hydrodynamic inundation modeling with an agent-based traffic-rescue simulation to examine adaptive resilience in urban traffic networks under compound flood-traffic conditions.

title: Early warning systems and anticipatory action: the fiscal case for pre-disaster financing

title: The complex links between food security, migration, and fragility in Northern Central America

title: Mental images of global warming in the Indian mind
content: Global warming is increasingly shaping daily life in India through extreme heat, changing rainfall patterns, floods, and droughts. But public understanding of “global warming” is not uniform.
snippet: Global warming is increasingly shaping daily life in India through extreme heat, changing rainfall patterns, floods, and droughts. But public understanding of “global warming” is not uniform.

title: Could geoengineering work to tamp down Super El Niños?
content: A 2019 Australian bushfire provided a rare opportunity to scientists by creating an effect analogous to what could be achieved through the geoengineering technique known as marine cloud brightening.
snippet: A 2019 Australian bushfire provided a rare opportunity to scientists by creating an effect analogous to what could be achieved through the geoengineering technique known as marine cloud brightening.

title: Building codes are not enough: Lessons from Venezuela’s earthquake

title: Adapting to climate-related health risks: the economic case for climate services for health

title: The ripple effects of industrial disasters

snippet: The East Palestine derailment illustrates an increasingly common reality: industrial facilities and critical infrastructure systems operate within highly interconnected environments.

title: Why heatwaves hit women harder
content: Biologically, women’s greater risk comes down to two things: the natural rise and fall of our hormone levels and our body's response to heat, which are different to men's, Dr Arif said.
snippet: Biologically, women’s greater risk comes down to two things: the natural rise and fall of our hormone levels and our body's response to heat, which are different to men's, Dr Arif said.

title: Quality in multilingual crisis communication: a reception-oriented perspective
